name: mod_lua multipart buffer overflow
description: A carefully crafted request body can cause a buffer overflow in the mod_lua multipart parser, leading to remote code execution.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.52 or later.

name: Path Traversal and Remote Code Execution
description: A path normalization flaw allows attackers to map URLs to files outside the expected document root. When combined with CGI, it enables RCE.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.50 or later.

name: Path Traversal fix bypass
description: The fix for CVE-2021-41773 in Apache 2.4.50 was insufficient. URL-encoded path traversal sequences can still bypass document root restrictions.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.51 or later.

name: Local privilege escalation via scoreboard
description: Apache HTTP Server worker processes can manipulate the scoreboard to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on graceful restart.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.39 or later.

name: Optionsbleed — OPTIONS memory leak
description: Use-after-free when processing the Allow header in OPTIONS responses can leak memory contents from the server process.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.28 or later.

name: HTTP Request Smuggling via mod_proxy
description: Some mod_proxy configurations allow HTTP Request Smuggling attacks, enabling request pipeline bypass and cache poisoning.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.56 or later.

name: mod_proxy SSRF via backend response
description: Vulnerability in mod_proxy allows SSRF attacks through crafted backend responses, potentially leading to local handler execution.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.62 or later.

name: mod_rewrite substitution encoding bypass
description: Substitution encoding issue in mod_rewrite allows execution of scripts in directories permitted by the configuration but not intended to be reachable.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.62 or later.

name: HTTP/2 stream handling DoS
description: An attacker opening an HTTP/2 connection with an initial window size of 0 can block handling of that connection indefinitely.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.58 or later.

name: HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frames DoS
description: Sending HTTP/2 requests with many CONTINUATION frames can cause excessive memory consumption in the server, leading to denial of service.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.59 or later.

name: HTTP Request Smuggling via r:parsebody
description: If a request body parsing error occurs, the connection is not closed properly, allowing HTTP request smuggling on subsequent requests.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.53 or later.

name: mod_status race condition heap overflow
description: A race condition in mod_status when using a threaded MPM can lead to a heap buffer overflow, potentially allowing code execution.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.10 or later.

name: ap_get_basic_auth_pw authentication bypass
description: Use of ap_get_basic_auth_pw() by third-party modules outside of the authentication phase may lead to authentication requirement bypass.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.2.33 or 2.4.26 or later.

name: mod_ssl null pointer dereference
description: Null pointer dereference in mod_ssl when third-party module calls ap_hook_process_connection() during an HTTP request to an HTTPS port.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.2.33 or 2.4.26 or later.

name: HTTP/2 push diary memory corruption
description: HTTP/2 push diary crash on specifically crafted HTTP/2 session, leading to memory corruption and potential denial of service.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.40 or later.

name: HTTP/2 session data use-after-free
description: Using fuzzed network input, the HTTP/2 session handling code can be made to access freed memory during connection shutdown.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.40 or later.

name: mod_mime buffer overread
description: In mod_mime, reading one byte past the end of a buffer when sending a malicious Content-Type response header can leak information.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.2.33 or 2.4.26 or later.

name: mod_proxy X-Forwarded-For header dropping
description: Apache HTTP Server may not send X-Forwarded-* headers to the origin server based on client-side Connection header hop-by-hop mechanism.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.54 or later.

name: ap_strcmp_match out-of-bounds read
description: Apache HTTP Server ap_strcmp_match() can be made to read one byte past the end of a buffer when called with a very large input.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.54 or later.

name: HTTP/2 Push Diary crash
description: A specially crafted Cache-Digest header in an HTTP/2 request triggers a crash when the server sends a PUSH frame, causing DoS.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.46 or later.

name: mod_proxy_uwsgi buffer overflow
description: Buffer overflow in mod_proxy_uwsgi allows remote code execution via a crafted request URI.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.44 or later.

name: mod_ssl access control bypass
description: In mod_ssl, a bug in TLSv1.3 renegotiation handling can be exploited to bypass configured access control restrictions.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.39 or later.

name: LimitXMLRequestBody integer overflow
description: Possible integer overflow in LimitXMLRequestBody set to > 350MB on 32-bit systems causes out-of-bounds writes.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.53 or later.

name: mod_macro out-of-bounds read
description: Out-of-bounds read in mod_macro when processing very long macro expansions can crash the server.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.58 or later.

name: Content-Type configuration regression
description: A regression in Apache 2.4.60 causes some content-type configurations to not be applied, potentially serving source code instead of processing.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTPD to 2.4.62 or later.
